title: clematis aristata subsp. cocculifolia
intro: Clematis aristata subsp. cocculifolia, also known as Mountain Clematis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion, due to its high content of glycosides and alkaloids. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, stems, and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
